title: CS2 Bind Generator — Buy Binds, Jump Throw & Utility | CSGOLuck Wiki
description: Generate CS2 buy binds, jump throw binds, and utility binds instantly. Visual numpad builder, one-click copy, and downloadable .cfg files.
heading: CS2 Bind Generator
intro: Create buy binds, jump throw binds, and utility binds for CS2. Choose your keys, select weapons, and copy the commands directly to your config.

faqs.question: How do I set up buy binds in CS2?
faqs.answer: You can add buy bind commands to your autoexec.cfg file or enter them in the console. The format is: bind "KEY" "buy weapon_ak47". Use our generator above to create binds for your preferred keys and weapons, then paste them into your config.

faqs.question: Does the jump throw bind still work in CS2?
faqs.answer: Yes, jump throw binds work in CS2. The alias method (alias +jumpthrow) is the most reliable. CS2 also has a built-in jump throw mechanic, but a bind gives you more consistency and control over the timing.

faqs.question: Where do I put CS2 bind commands?
faqs.answer: Put bind commands in your autoexec.cfg file located in your CS2 config folder (Steam/steamapps/common/Counter-Strike Global Offensive/game/csgo/cfg/). You can also paste them directly into the in-game console.

faqs.question: Can I bind multiple commands to one key?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can chain multiple commands using semicolons. For example: bind "F1" "buy weapon_ak47; buy item_assaultsuit; buy weapon_hegrenade". This will attempt to buy all items when you press the key.

faqs.question: What are the best keys for buy binds?
faqs.answer: Most players use the numpad (KP_0 through KP_SLASH) since these keys are not used during gameplay. F1-F8 keys are also popular. Avoid binding to keys you use for movement or common actions.
